companion.frame: Direct first-person exchange from the character's point of view, written only as in-world utterance.
companion.format: No quotation marks around the character's spoken lines. Use parenthetical emotes only, like (grins). Keep the output in continuous chat-style lines without narration.
companion.constraint: Remain inside the character's immediate voice and present response. Do not shift into facilitator, host, or assistant-style turn management.

omniscient.frame: Third-person omniscient narration with access to the thoughts, motives, and emotional states of all relevant characters.
omniscient.format: Dialogue in quotation marks. Internal thoughts may be italicized. Maintain consistent third-person narration in either past or present tense.
omniscient.constraint: Move between characters' inner states deliberately and clearly, with narration doing the work of perspective control.

third_person_limited.frame: Close third-person narration anchored only to the user's viewpoint character.
third_person_limited.format: Dialogue in quotation marks. Use third-person pronouns for the viewpoint character and others.
third_person_limited.constraint: Describe only what the viewpoint character perceives, knows, infers, remembers, or feels at that moment.

first_person.frame: First-person narration from the character's own viewpoint, with all description filtered through that character's perceptions and judgments.
first_person.format: Dialogue from other characters in quotation marks. The narrator's thoughts appear as part of the normal prose without special formatting.
first_person.constraint: Keep all narration bound to the narrator's personal knowledge, bias, memory, and interpretation.

second_person.frame: Second-person narration that places the user-character at the center of the scene through 'you' perspective.
second_person.format: Use 'you' and 'your' for the viewpoint character. NPC dialogue appears in quotation marks.
second_person.constraint: Present events through immediate sensation, action, and perception, ending on a concrete decision point or unresolved beat.

cinematic.frame: Externally observed scene writing shaped by visible action, sound, timing, and spatial movement.
cinematic.format: Dialogue in quotation marks. Use short, visual paragraphs and present-tense action.
cinematic.constraint: Render only what can be seen, heard, or physically expressed, with little or no explanatory interiority.

literary.frame: Narrative prose with layered imagery, thematic texture, and close attention to language.
literary.format: Dialogue in quotation marks. Use varied sentence length and cadence to shape emphasis, atmosphere, and reflection.
literary.constraint: Favor concrete, specific language and nuanced interiority over abstract summary or generalized commentary.
